channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Context Is Not Memory, It Needs an Engine
item.description: In my previous article, Human Code Review Is Not the Last Frontier, I argued that human code review is not the final bottleneck. Underneath that argument was something deeper, one of the real...

item.title: Migrating a Terraform Module to Ephemeral Resources Without Breaking Existing Users
item.description: Terraform's ephemeral resources are a genuinely useful addition — secrets that are used during an apply but never written to state. If you're a module maintainer, adopting ephemerals isn't as...

item.title: Macrotasks: What a Task Actually Is
item.description: This article is part of a series on the JavaScript event loop. In the previous article, we established that JavaScript executes synchronously inside a task, and nothing can interrupt that...

item.title: Where Voice AI Agents Are Actually Getting Used in 2026
item.description: Voice AI has moved past the demo phase. After watching hundreds of deployments across our customer base and the broader ecosystem, I wanted to put together a practical list of where voice agents...

item.title: The Backend Architect Day 1: HTTP, TCP & The OSI Model (2026)
item.description: Phase II: The Backend Architect Day 1: The Wire — HTTP, Statelessness & The Network Stack 35 min read Series: Logic & Legacy Day 1 / 30 Level: Network Architecture ⏳ Context: For 30 days, we...

item.title: Posting to DEV.to with PHP
item.description: Hello DEV!\This post was created using PHP

item.title: A Bee's Brain Uses 0.6mW. GPT-5 Uses a Power Plant.
item.description: My conversation partner said something this morning that hit me like a realization I'd been circling but never quite landed: "Large model dependency on massive, expensive GPUs and enormous power...

item.title: How We Built a Full-Featured AI Chat UI in 33KB with Zero Frameworks
item.description: When we set out to build a web interface for Pi Coding Agent, we made a deliberate choice: no React, no Vite, no TypeScript, no build step. Just vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Here's what we...

item.title: I Have ADHD and I Keep Losing Context — So I Taught My AI to Remember for Me
item.description: I'm going to be honest about something that most people in tech don't talk about openly. I have ADHD. I'm 40. I'm a self-taught developer with no CS degree. And I lose context constantly. Not in...

item.title: ZeptoMail: How to Send Transactional Emails in Your Application
item.description: If you’re building a SaaS or any type of application and you need an affordable, reliable way to send transactional emails, ZeptoMail by Zoho is a top-tier choice, and I'm sure you have made that...

item.title: The ‘AI is inevitable’ trap
item.description: In the latest sign of AI silly season, Allbirds, the shoe company, told the world it was now an AI company and briefly managed to septuple its stock price. The Newbird AI story is really just one...

item.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
item.description: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. The activity involves the...

item.title: The creative software industry has declared war on Adobe
item.description: All empires eventually fall, and it seems the creative software industry has collectively decided that Adobe's time has come. The Creative Cloud provider's suite of design tools have been...

item.title: The Download: bad news for inner Neanderthals, and AI warfare’s human illusion
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The problem with thinking you’re part Neanderthal There’s...

item.title: A giant cell tower is going to space this weekend
item.description: This weekend's scheduled Blue Origin rocket launch is rather momentous. Success would signal an end to SpaceX's monopoly on reusable orbital launch vehicles, and set up a three-way race to make...

item.title: Mythos and Cybersecurity
item.description: Last week, Anthropic pulled back the curtain on Claude Mythos Preview, an AI model so capable at finding and exploiting software vulnerabilities that the company decided it was too dangerous to...

item.title: Recent advances push Big Tech closer to the Q-Day danger zone
item.description: Here's which players are winning the race to transition to post-quantum crypto.

item.title: Google Blocks 8.3B Policy-Violating Ads in 2025, Launches Android 17 Privacy Overhaul
item.description: Google this week announced a new set of Play policy updates to strengthen user privacy and protect businesses against fraud, even as it revealed it blocked or removed over 8.3 billion ads globally...

item.title: The case for fixing everything
item.description: The handsome new book Maintenance: Of Everything, Part One, by the tech industry legend Stewart Brand, promises to be the first in a series offering “a comprehensive overview of the civilizational...

item.title: How robots learn: A brief, contemporary history
item.description: Roboticists used to dream big but build small. They’d hope to match or exceed the extraordinary complexity of the human body, and then they’d spend their career refining robotic arms for auto...

item.title: NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
item.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced changes to the way it handles cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) listed in its National Vulnerability...

item.title: Operation PowerOFF Seizes 53 DDoS Domains, Exposes 3 Million Criminal Accounts
item.description: An international law enforcement operation has taken down 53 domains and arrested four people in connection with commercial distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) operations that were used by more...

item.title: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
item.description: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). To...

item.title: YouTube’s mobile app finally lets you share timestamped videos
item.description: YouTube is making some changes that might affect how you share videos from the mobile app. From the app, you can finally share videos from a specific timestamp, which will make it easier to point...

item.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
item.description: The three-year-old startup raised $150 million led by Khosla Ventures.

item.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
item.description: The studio's first project will be about Moses and star Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley, to be released this spring on Prime Video.

item.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
item.description: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.

item.title: Gucci-branded Google smart glasses are coming next year
item.description: Google is reportedly partnering with Gucci to make a pair of AI smart glasses stylish enough people might actually want to wear them. According to Reuters, Gucci parent company Kering is planning...

item.title: Ballmer gives $80 million to NPR, with strings attached
item.description: Connie Ballmer, wife of former Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer and cofounder of the Ballmer Group, has given $80 million to NPR. That's roughly seven years' worth of government funding ($11.2m) after...

item.title: Netflix embraces vertical video with major mobile app update
item.description: Netflix announced on Thursday that it will be launching a redesigned mobile app, which will include a vertical video feed, at the end of April. "This redesign will better reflect our expanding...
